title: Arien Yage

{{Quote|Look for Captain Arien Yage of the frigate ''Widowmaker''. She used to serve with me on the ''Chimaera''; if she survived Bastion, she'll listen to you.|[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] to [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]|<ref name="Force Heretic I: Remnant" />}}
'''Arien Yage''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] commissioned as a [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] in the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]] of the [[Imperial Remnant]]. During the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], she held command of the Imperial frigate [[Widowmaker (frigate)|''Widowmaker'']], and following the [[Battle of Borosk (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Battle of Borosk]] she and her crew joined the expedition of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], in his attempts to locate [[Zonama Sekot/Legends|Zonama Sekot]], under the orders of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]].

==Biography==
===Yuuzhan Vong War===
Prior to the [[Battle of Ithor]], Captain Yage was assigned to the [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] as its [[Commander/Legends|Commander]].<ref name="The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin">''[[The New Jedi Order: Dark Tide II: Ruin]]'', 277.</ref> Her relationship with [[Supreme Commander (Galactic Empire)/Legends|Supreme Commander]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] appears to have been quite personal; through interactions with him she consistently referred to, and at times addressed him by his personal name, "Gilad,"<ref name="Force Heretic I: Remnant" /> a highly unorthodox form of communication between a Navy [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] and a [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]].

Indeed, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Pellaeon appeared to hold considerable trust in Yage, as he advised the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] aboard the ''[[Jade Shadow]]'' who appeared towards the end of the [[Battle of Bastion]] to seek out Captain Yage at [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]]. Yage held discussions with [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], while reluctantly awaiting orders from the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Council of Moffs]], who hoped to fill the power vacuum that appeared to form with the supposed death of Pellaeon.

After learning that Pellaeon had indeed survived, Yage was contacted by [[General/Legends|General]] [[Berrida]], who like the Council was unaware of the Grand Admiral's survival. As a show of faith, Solo aided Yage by answering the communication from Berrida instead of Captain Yage, so as to stall the Council's attempts to bring her under their command. With Pellaeon in a critical condition, he was vulnerable and keeping his survival secret became a priority for Yage.

While Pellaeon recovered in a [[Bacta tank/Legends|bacta tank]], Yage became somewhat of an ally to the Jedi. Pellaeon soon revealed his survival to the [[Moff/Legends|Moffs]] who in fear of his wrath at their intrigue hastened to comply with his commands to flee for [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] and ready for battle with the Yuuzhan Vong. During the battle Yage acted as Pellaeon's personal assistant and following the Imperial victory, he ordered her to aid the Jedi in their expedition, by adding the ''Widowmaker'' to their arsenal.

====Aiding the Jedi====
Later, while a portion of the expedition team landed on the planet of [[Munlali Mafir]] in the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]], Yage remained in orbit aboard the ''Widowmaker''. When Master Skywalker and his ground team returned from an unsuccessful mission to the planet, Yage contacted him to make him aware of an approaching [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] force. Soon the Chiss made contact with Yage personally, demanding she arrange an audience with Skywalker.

During the audience with the Chiss, Yage was astounded by the Chiss commanders rudeness, and advised against complying with her suggestion to journey to [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]]. However, the task force did indeed venture to the Chiss homeworld, where the Jedi discovered the whereabouts of Zonama Sekot.

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|I toyed with actually suggesting Airen ''[sic]'' Yage, but in the end suggested Daala, with the cavet that she was a bit of a loon who gassed Moffs.|James Gilmer recalls advising Karen Traviss on a character to resurrect for her 2008 novel ''Revelation''|<ref name="Gilmer1">{{JCFCite|url=posts/28500914|board=Literature|thread=Daala, you are such a pain: Why We Don't Take Daala Seriously (Invincible Spoilers)|user=James Gilmer|date=2008-05-25|time=11:55 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200730082706/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/daala-you-are-such-a-pain-why-we-dont-take-daala-seriously-invincible-spoilers.28499562/page-3}}</ref>}}
At one point in ''Force Heretic II'', Yage is referenced as "[[Todra Mayn|Mayn]]" following a shift in the novel from the plot involving [[Bakura/Legends|Bakura]].

While writing her [[2008]] novel ''[[Legacy of the Force: Revelation]]'', author [[Karen Traviss]] was seeking an unrepentant Imperial character to resurrect as part of a [[Wikipedia:Drake's Drum|Drake's Drum]], ship-captain-returned-from-the-dead theme. She wanted to use a female character whose unrepentant style would contrast with the character of [[Cha Niathal]], and she turned to her friend James Gilmer for advice. Gilmer considered suggesting both Arien Yage<ref name="Gilmer1" /> and Moff [[Leonia Tavira]]<ref name="Gilmer2">{{JCFCite|url=posts/28502026|board=Literature|thread=Daala, you are such a pain: Why We Don't Take Daala Seriously (Invincible Spoilers)|user=James Gilmer|date=2008-05-25|time=6:24 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200730082729/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/daala-you-are-such-a-pain-why-we-dont-take-daala-seriously-invincible-spoilers.28499562/page-4}}</ref> before ultimately choosing the character of Admiral [[Natasi Daala|Daala]].<ref name="Gilmer1" />
